intro: Cumulopuntia leucophaea, commonly known as the White Pincushion Cactus, is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cumulopuntia leucophaea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues.
